parentQuestion: The eye muscle that rotates the eye upward and turns the eye laterally is the ________.

scaffolds.q: The eye is moved by six extraocular muscles.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! 4 rectus muscles + 2 oblique muscles control eye position.

scaffolds.q: The RECTUS muscles move the eye in straight directions (up, down, medial, lateral).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Superior rectus (up), inferior rectus (down), medial (nose), lateral (temple).

scaffolds.q: The OBLIQUE muscles have diagonal/rotational actions.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Their insertion angles cause complex movements.
Correct! Superior oblique moves eye DOWN and OUT. CN IV innervates it.
Yes! Inferior oblique moves eye UP and OUT. CN III innervates it.

scaffolds.q: The inferior oblique is the ONLY muscle that elevates AND abducts.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Superior rectus elevates but ADDUCTS. Inferior oblique elevates and ABDUCTS.

scaffolds.q: Lateral rectus only abducts (lateral movement). It doesn't elevate.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Lateral rectus is purely horizontal movement.

scaffolds.q: Elevates + abducts = inferior oblique (up and laterally).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The inferior oblique's insertion angle produces both movements.

scaffolds.q: The answer is INFERIOR OBLIQUE - elevates (up) and abducts (lateral).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Not superior oblique (depresses, not elevates), not lateral rectus (abducts only, no elevation), not medial rectus (adducts, not abducts).
